title: eriochilus dilatatus subsp. dilatatus
intro: Eriochilus dilatatus subsp. dilatatus, also known as the Hairy Fingers Orchid, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. This plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The Hairy Fingers Orchid has been reported to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and alleviate certain health conditions.
